channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from trusted sources

item.title: US and Iran Near Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A US-Iran agreement to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz could be signed during next week's G7 summit, with both sides expected to extend their ceasefire by approximately two months.

item.title: UAE Denies $3 Billion Transfer to Iran
item.description: The UAE categorically denied reports that it had transferred $3 billion to Iran as part of a deal to end attacks on the Gulf country, amid ongoing diplomatic negotiations.

item.title: Explosion Reported in Southern Iranian Port City of Sirik
item.description: Explosions were reported late Friday in the area of Sirik port in southern Iran, according to Iranian state television, in a region near the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UK Sets 2027 Deadline for Ban on Russian-Derived Fuel
item.description: The UK announced it will fully phase in its ban on diesel and jet fuel produced from Russian crude oil by 2027, tightening sanctions on Moscow's energy exports.

item.title: Netanyahu Faces Bitter Pill as Trump's Iran Deal Nears
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, who had hoped the Iran war could spur regime change in Tehran, now faces a US-Iran deal that could complicate Israel's strategic position.

item.title: China Confirms Arrest of US Citizen Suspected of Spying
item.description: China confirmed the arrest of a US citizen on suspicion of espionage amid heightened tensions between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: UAE to Unlock Frozen Iranian Funds Amid US Ceasefire Push
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is moving to unlock frozen Iranian funds as part of a broader diplomatic effort to secure a ceasefire between the US and Iran.

item.title: Justice Department Approves Paramount Acquisition of Warner Bros
item.description: The US Justice Department approved Paramount's $110 billion bid to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery, clearing a major regulatory hurdle for the media mega-merger.

item.title: Ukraine to Request $20 Billion from Allies to Maintain Momentum Against Russia
item.description: Ukraine will ask its allies for $20 billion in additional funding to maintain military momentum against Russia, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Pope Leo Warns Europe's Migrant Traffickers: Repent or Face Hell
item.description: Pope Leo issued a stern warning to migrant traffickers operating in Europe, urging them to repent or face eternal damnation for exploiting vulnerable people.

item.title: Iran War Limbo: US Weighs Next Moves After Ceasefire
item.description: The conflict between the US and Iran remains in a state of uncertainty as both sides weigh their next diplomatic and military moves following the recent ceasefire.

item.title: Iran Loosens Strait of Hormuz Stranglehold
item.description: Iran has begun loosening its grip on the Strait of Hormuz as diplomatic efforts to end hostilities with the US gain momentum, offering hope for restored global oil shipments.

item.title: NATO and US Europe Defense Posture Under Review
item.description: NATO and US defense commitments in Europe face renewed scrutiny as allies weigh how to address evolving security challenges from Russia and beyond.

item.title: US Navy Increases Submarine Operations Amid Global Arms Race
item.description: The US Navy is ramping up submarine deployments as underwater capabilities become increasingly critical in the global military competition.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Opens at $150 After Record-Busting Debut
item.description: SpaceX shares opened at $150 in its highly anticipated IPO, marking the largest debut in market history and propelling Elon Musk toward potential trillionaire status.

item.title: UK Economy Contracted 0.1% in April
item.description: The UK economy shrank by 0.1% in April according to official data, raising concerns about the country's economic trajectory.

item.title: US to Provide $50 Million to Counter Ebola Outbreak
item.description: The US government will provide $50 million in funding to help contain the ongoing Ebola outbreak, bolstering international health security efforts.

item.title: Russia Building Infrastructure for Major Troop Movements
item.description: Russia is constructing new military infrastructure that could support large-scale troop deployments, raising concerns about future offensive operations.

item.title: Violence Erupts at DRC Rally Against Presidential Term Limit Change
item.description: Violence broke out at a rally in the Democratic Republic of Congo opposing proposed changes to presidential term limits, highlighting political tensions.

item.title: US Deports Iranian Pro-Democracy Activist to Central African Republic
item.description: A US deportation flight carrying an Iranian pro-democracy activist and other migrants is headed to the Central African Republic, raising human rights concerns.

item.title: Astronomers Fear Orbital Data Centers Will Interfere with Observations
item.description: Space-based data centers planned for low Earth orbit could interfere with ground-based astronomical observations, scientists warn.

item.title: Congress Turns Down Lights on US Surveillance Authorities
item.description: US lawmakers are scaling back surveillance authorities amid concerns about civil liberties and government overreach.

item.title: Hell's Army: Inside the Fight Against Transnational Criminal Networks
item.description: An investigation into the global effort to combat transnational criminal networks operating across borders.

item.title: US-China Tech Race: Beijing's Fusion Ambitions Threaten American Lead
item.description: China's EAST fusion reactor is targeting ignition by 2027, with a second project poised to generate electricity, potentially threatening US leadership in fusion energy.
